# The AI Encyclopedia

> A free, open, interactive encyclopedia of AI: machine learning from first
> principles, the complete LLM field manual, prompting, and agent engineering —
> with runnable Python and 70+ live instruments. https://llm-manual.vercel.app

## Volumes

- [VOL I · 01 · Learning from Data](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/01-learning-from-data.html): Everything in this encyclopedia — regression lines, neural networks, trillion-parameter language models — is a single idea wearing progressi
- [VOL I · 02 · Linear Regression &amp; Gradient Descent](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/02-linear-regression.html): One model — a weighted sum. One loss — squared error. One algorithm — step downhill. Linear regression is the smallest machine on which the 
- [VOL I · 03 · Classification: Logistic &amp; Softmax](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/03-classification.html): Chapter 02 predicted numbers; the world mostly asks for choices — spam or not, benign or malignant, which of 100,000 tokens comes next. The 
- [VOL I · 04 · Trees, Forests &amp; Neighbors](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/04-trees-and-neighbors.html): Not every model is a curve bent by gradient descent. This chapter is about methods that keep the training data instead of compressing it awa
- [VOL I · 05 · Clustering &amp; Dimensionality](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/05-unsupervised.html): Every method so far was handed the right answers. This chapter takes them away. With no labels, the model must find structure the data carri
- [VOL I · 06 · Generalization: Bias, Variance &amp; Regularization](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/06-generalization.html): Any model with enough knobs can score perfectly on data it has already seen — that is memorization, and it is worth nothing. The only error 
- [VOL I · 07 · Neural Networks: The MLP](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/07-neural-networks.html): A linear model can draw exactly one flat boundary, and four points are enough to defeat it. The fix is almost embarrassingly small: stack tw
- [VOL I · 08 · Backpropagation &amp; Optimization](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/ml/08-backpropagation.html): Chapter 07 left a network with thirty-three knobs and one number telling it how wrong it is. This chapter is the algorithm that turns that o
- [VOL II · 01 · Foundations](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/01-foundations.html): Strip away the scale and the engineering, and a large language model is one function : given a sequence of tokens, output a probability dist
- [VOL II · 02 · The Transformer](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/02-transformer.html): Every frontier model is a decoder-only transformer : a stack of identical blocks reading and writing a shared workspace called the residual 
- [VOL II · 03 · Attention](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/03-attention.html): Attention is a differentiable soft lookup : every position publishes what it has (keys, values) and what it wants (queries), and information
- [VOL II · 04 · Pre-training](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/04-pretraining.html): Pre-training is the act of spending a compute budget — months of time on tens of thousands of accelerators — to push cross-entropy as low as
- [VOL II · 05 · Post-training](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/05-posttraining.html): A base model knows; it does not behave . Post-training is the comparatively tiny but decisive stage that converts a next-token predictor int
- [VOL II · 06 · Fine-tuning](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/06-finetuning.html): Adapting a pre-trained giant to your task is mostly a question of which parameters you allow to move . This chapter covers the spectrum from
- [VOL II · 07 · Compression](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/07-compression.html): Generating a token means streaming every weight through the chip . At decode time LLMs are memory-bandwidth-bound, so the bits each weight o
- [VOL II · 08 · Inference &amp; Deployment](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/08-inference.html): Serving is its own discipline with its own physics: a compute-bound prefill followed by a memory-bound decode , billions of dollars riding o
- [VOL II · 09 · The Frontier](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/09-frontier.html): The dense decoder of Chapters 02–03 is now the baseline , not the frontier. Production flagships route tokens through expert subnetworks, re
- [VOL II · 10 · Diffusion](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/10-diffusion.html): The second great family of generative models works nothing like next-token prediction: destroy data with noise, then learn to walk the destr
- [VOL II · Capstone · The Full Stack](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/chapters/capstone.html): Ten chapters compress into two instruments. First, design a frontier model — every slider here invokes an equation you have already met, fro
- [VOL III · 01 · How Models Read Prompts](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/01-how-prompts-work.html): A prompt is not a message to a mind. It is the condition in a conditional probability: every token you write reshapes the distribution over 
- [VOL III · 02 · The Scaffold: Role · Task · Context · Format · Constraints](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/02-the-scaffold.html): A weak prompt is rarely missing cleverness — it is missing information. The scaffold is a five-part checklist that forces every piece of con
- [VOL III · 03 · Show, Don't Tell: Few-Shot &amp; Examples](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/03-few-shot.html): Instructions describe a task; examples demonstrate it — and the model was trained on demonstration, not description. Across nearly every pro
- [VOL III · 04 · Reasoning Controls: CoT to Effort Dials](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/04-reasoning.html): For two years, five words — “let's think step by step” — were the highest-leverage edit in applied AI. Then models trained with reinforcemen
- [VOL III · 05 · Structured Output &amp; Tool-Ready Prompts](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/05-structured-output.html): The moment a model's output feeds a program instead of a person, formatting stops being style and becomes contract . This chapter is the lad
- [VOL III · 06 · Self-Critique, Red Teams &amp; Councils](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/06-adversarial.html): Everything a model emits in one pass is a draft — fluent, confident, and unexamined. The techniques in this chapter exploit one asymmetry: m
- [VOL III · 07 · Evaluation &amp; The Prompt Lab](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/07-evaluation-lab.html): Six chapters of this volume have made claim after claim: scaffolds beat bare asks, examples beat adjectives, critique loops catch what singl
- [VOL III · ⌘ · The Pattern Library](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/prompting/patterns.html): The seven chapters behind you are organized by technique. This page is the same volume rotated ninety degrees — organized by the work itself
- [VOL IV · 01 · From Chat to Agents: The Loop](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/agents/01-the-agentic-loop.html): A chatbot emits an answer and the episode ends. An agent emits an action , watches what the world does about it, and gets to try again — and
- [VOL IV · 02 · Context Engineering](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/agents/02-context-engineering.html): Prompt engineering optimized one string for one call. Agents broke that frame: across a fifty-step loop, the question is no longer how to ph
- [VOL IV · 03 · Tool Design &amp; MCP](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/agents/03-tools-and-mcp.html): A tool is the API between intelligence and the world — and the model never sees your code, only its surface . The name, the description, and
- [VOL IV · 04 · Harness Engineering](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/agents/04-harness-engineering.html): A capable model wired straight into a shell is not a product; it is an incident report with a future timestamp. The harness — sandbox, permi
- [VOL IV · 05 · Loop Engineering &amp; Multi-Agent Patterns](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/agents/05-loop-engineering.html): An agent is a loop, and a loop is a product of probabilities: fifty steps at 99% each is a coin flip. Models improve on someone else's sched
- [VOL IV · 06 · Evals, Observability &amp; Cost](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/agents/06-evals-observability.html): Five chapters of this volume taught you to build agents. This one teaches you to know whether they work, why they fail, and what they cost —

## Practice

- [The Gym](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/gym/index.html): drills, numeric problems and Python katas, graded client-side.

## Full content

- [llms-full.txt](https://llm-manual.vercel.app/llms-full.txt): the complete text of every chapter in one file — load it as context.
